Hi Flavia! We have one minute before the next talk. Can you present your project?
Of course! I am a consultant at Sebrae, and I help small businesses. Here is my pitch.
Great. What problem do you solve?
Many small businesses have a problem: they cannot reach global markets. But we have a solution.
Interesting! And what is the benefit?
The result is more sales and more jobs. I love this work because it helps real families.
That was a clear pitch, and it was only one minute!
Thank you, Lucas! A short message is powerful because people remember it.
Let me give you my pitch in one minute.
We have a problem, but we have a solution.
This idea works because it solves a real problem.
The main benefit is more sales and more jobs.
My message is simple, but it is powerful.

Hello, my name is Flavia, and I am a consultant at Sebrae. Let me give you my pitch in one minute. Many small businesses in Brazil have a big problem: they do not know how to reach global markets. But we have a solution. We help them prepare, and we connect them to international partners. The result is more sales and more jobs. I love this project because it helps real people and real families. Thank you!

Good afternoon. Here is one simple tip for a great one-minute pitch. First, start with the problem, because people listen when they understand the problem. Then give your solution, and explain the main benefit. Keep your message short and clear, because you only have one minute. And remember: speak with energy, but stay calm. A good pitch is simple, but it is powerful. Good luck!

First, Flavia says her name and her job at Sebrae. Then she explains the problem: small businesses cannot reach global markets. Next, she gives the solution and the main benefit. After that, she shares the result: more sales and more jobs. Finally, she says why she loves the project, because it helps real people.
